items.title: Conflict Resolution Wizard
items.description: When a sync would overwrite a target harness file that has local modifications, present a diff-based merge UI in the terminal instead of silently overwriting or failing. Users who experiment with Gemini-specific tweaks shouldn't lose that work on next sync — they need a way to preserve intentional divergence or resolve conflicts deliberately.

items.title: Feature Parity Gap Report
items.description: After a sync, generate a human-readable report showing which Claude Code capabilities have no equivalent in each target (e.g., 'Cursor has no agent concept — 3 agents were skipped'). Users waste time debugging why behaviors differ across harnesses without knowing what was silently dropped during translation.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Let users define named profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that specify different sync targets, sections, and overrides per context. A developer working on a client project shouldn't have personal skills or MCPs bleed into that workspace — profiles give them clean separation without manual toggling.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP servers in the user's Claude Code config are natively supported, partially supported, or unsupported by each target harness. Users don't know which MCPs will work in Codex vs Gemini until they try — a pre-sync compatibility check prevents silent tool failures in other harnesses.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: A /sync --dry-run command that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing any files. Users with large config setups are afraid to sync because they don't know what will change — a preview builds confidence before committing.

items.title: Rule Tagging for Selective Sync
items.description: Allow users to annotate sections of CLAUDE.md with tags like <!-- sync:codex-only --> or <!-- sync:skip --> that control which targets receive each block. A user might have Claude-specific tool-use instructions that would be confusing noise in AGENTS.md — tags give surgical control without maintaining separate files.

items.title: New Harness Onboarding Generator
items.description: When a new harness is detected (e.g., user installs Cursor for the first time), auto-generate a bootstrap sync that creates all target configs from scratch with a single command. The current first-sync experience requires knowing the right commands — a guided onboarding flow eliminates that friction.

items.title: Settings Translation Explainer
items.description: For each synced setting, show a tooltip/explanation of how it was translated (e.g., 'Claude Code allowedTools → Codex allowedCommands: filtered to shell commands only'). Users are confused when a permission works in Claude Code but behaves differently elsewhere — transparency in translation builds trust.

items.title: Git Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook mode that blocks commits if the sync state is stale (i.e., Claude Code config has changed but targets are out of date). Teams using shared repos can enforce that all harness configs stay consistent before code is committed — prevents drift from sneaking into the repo.

items.title: Team Sync Server / Shared Config Hub
items.description: A lightweight server mode where a team's Claude Code config is the authoritative source and teammates pull from it on connect. Individual devs shouldn't have to manually sync when the team lead updates CLAUDE.md — a pull-on-open model keeps everyone current automatically.

items.title: Context-Aware Auto-Sync Triggers
items.description: Auto-sync only relevant sections based on what changed — editing a skill syncs skills, saving CLAUDE.md syncs rules, adding an MCP syncs MCP configs. The current hook fires a full sync on any change, which is wasteful and slow for large configs. Surgical triggers make auto-sync feel instant.

items.title: Config Snapshot Timeline
items.description: Maintain a local history of config snapshots with timestamps, allowing one-command rollback to any previous state across all targets. Users who accidentally break their config have no safety net today — a snapshot timeline turns rollback from a nerve-wracking manual operation into a trivial command.

items.title: Harness Override Files
items.description: Support CLAUDE.codex.md, CLAUDE.gemini.md etc. that are merged (not replaced) with the base CLAUDE.md when syncing to that specific target. Power users want 95% shared config with 5% harness-specific tuning — override files give them that without forking their entire config.

items.title: GitHub Actions / CI Sync Validator
items.description: A GitHub Action that runs in CI to verify that all harness config files in the repo are in sync with CLAUDE.md, failing the build if drift is detected. Teams with multiple contributors can't rely on everyone running sync manually — CI enforcement makes drift a build failure, not a surprise.

items.title: Natural Language Sync Filter
items.description: Let users describe what to sync in plain English: /sync 'only my Python rules and the filesystem MCP to Cursor'. Translates to the right flags internally. Non-power-users shouldn't need to memorize flags and section names — natural language input lowers the barrier to selective sync.

items.title: Plugin Manifest Sync
items.description: Sync installed Claude Code plugins to equivalent extensions or config in target harnesses where a mapping exists (e.g., a Claude Code plugin that wraps a VS Code extension could activate the same extension in Cursor). Plugin investment shouldn't be lost when switching harnesses — capability portability extends to the ecosystem.

items.title: Sync Health Score Dashboard
items.description: A single numeric score (0-100) per target harness representing how faithfully the sync translated the source config, with drilldown into what dragged the score down. Users need a quick signal for 'is my Gemini config as capable as my Claude config?' — a health score gives them that at a glance.

items.title: Secret Scrubbing Before Sync
items.description: Detect and redact API keys, tokens, and credentials in MCP server env vars before writing them to target configs, replacing with placeholder env var references. Users copy MCP configs that contain real secrets — silently syncing those to a repo-tracked file is a security incident waiting to happen.

items.title: Multi-Workspace Broadcast Sync
items.description: Sync a single source config to multiple project directories at once, with per-project filter overrides. Developers maintaining 10+ repos shouldn't have to cd into each one and run sync — broadcast mode applies changes everywhere in a single command.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, append a human-readable entry to a SYNC_CHANGELOG.md describing what changed, when, and for which targets. When something breaks in another harness, users have no audit trail — a changelog makes it trivial to correlate 'Cursor stopped following my rules' with 'I updated CLAUDE.md two days ago'.

items.title: Third-Party Adapter Plugin API
items.description: Define a stable adapter interface that lets the community write sync adapters for harnesses HarnessSync doesn't officially support (e.g., Continue.dev, Cline, Roo). The long tail of AI coding tools is growing fast — a plugin API turns HarnessSync into a platform instead of a fixed list of targets.

items.title: IDE Extension Settings Sync
items.description: Extend sync to write relevant settings into VS Code / JetBrains AI extension config files (GitHub Copilot instructions, Cline system prompts) in addition to CLI harnesses. Most developers use both CLI tools and IDE extensions — HarnessSync stops at the CLI boundary today, leaving half the ecosystem unaddressed.

items.title: Unified MCP Proxy Layer
items.description: Run a local MCP proxy that all harnesses connect to, so MCP server config only needs to live in one place and HarnessSync manages routing. Managing separate MCP configs per harness creates version skew — a proxy layer means adding an MCP server once makes it available everywhere instantly.

items.title: Rule Effectiveness Scorer
items.description: Analyze git history and AI session logs to score which CLAUDE.md rules correlate with fewer follow-up corrections, and surface underperforming rules for revision. Users write rules but never know if they actually change model behavior — effectiveness scoring closes the feedback loop between writing and impact.

items.title: Bootstrap from Awesome-CLAUDE Repos
items.description: A /sync-import command that fetches a community CLAUDE.md template from GitHub (e.g., awesome-claude-md repos) and merges it into the user's config with a review step. New users stare at a blank CLAUDE.md — a community template library gives them a running start based on their tech stack.

items.title: PR Comment Sync Diff Poster
items.description: When a PR modifies CLAUDE.md or config files, post a comment showing the projected diff across all target harnesses. Reviewers can't tell whether a CLAUDE.md change will have unintended downstream effects on Codex or Gemini — a PR comment with the harness diffs makes review informed.

items.title: Scheduled Drift Alerts
items.description: Periodically check if target harness configs have drifted from what HarnessSync last wrote (e.g., someone manually edited AGENTS.md) and send a desktop notification or terminal warning. Silent drift is the core enemy — proactive alerts catch it before it causes confusing behavior differences.

items.title: Config Complexity Analyzer
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md for redundancy, contradiction, and bloat — flagging rules that conflict, duplicate each other, or are too vague to be actionable. Config files grow organically and accumulate noise; users have no tool to audit quality. A complexity report helps them keep configs lean and effective.

items.title: Universal Skill Format Standard
items.description: Define and publish a harness-agnostic skill format that HarnessSync can translate to/from all target formats, and advocate for adoption. Each harness has its own skill schema — users writing skills for Claude Code can't share them with Gemini users. A universal format makes skills truly portable across the ecosystem.

items.title: .env-Aware MCP Config Sync
items.description: When syncing MCP server configs, automatically resolve env var names from the project's .env file and write parameterized references instead of hardcoded values. Developers share repos with teammates who have different API keys — .env-aware sync writes portable configs that work for everyone on the team.

items.title: Cross-Harness Benchmark Comparison
items.description: Run a standard set of prompts against each synced harness and report which follows the shared rules most faithfully, effectively benchmarking rule adherence per harness. Users suspect one harness follows their rules better than another but have no data — benchmarks make this measurable and actionable.

items.title: Team vs Personal Config Namespacing
items.description: Distinguish between team-level config (shared, in repo) and personal config (local, in home dir) and sync each to the right scope in target harnesses. Developers on teams need their personal quirks (preferred variable naming, personal MCPs) not to pollute the shared team rules that go into AGENTS.md.

items.title: Missing Capability Wishlist Tracker
items.description: Track which Claude Code capabilities users most often wish were available in each target harness, and generate a formatted feature request template for the target's issue tracker. Users feel the pain of missing capabilities but don't know if others share it — aggregated wishlists help them advocate effectively with harness maintainers.

items.title: Offline Sync Queue
items.description: Queue sync operations when target directories are unavailable (network drives, remote mounts, unmounted external disks) and replay them when the path becomes accessible. Users with remote project dirs or NAS-mounted workspaces lose sync events silently — a queue ensures nothing is dropped.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Suggester
items.description: Analyze the user's codebase (language, frameworks, test patterns) and suggest CLAUDE.md rules they're likely missing based on community best practices for their stack. New users don't know what rules to write — a context-aware suggester turns blank-page paralysis into a curated starting point.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
